items.title: Johor voters in Singapore frustrated by lack of leave to vote
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Several Johor voters working in Singapore have expressed disappointment over their inability to cast their ballots in the state election today after failing to secure leave from their employers. Meanwhile, lab assistant V. Rabindran, 35, said he had missed voting several times throughout his employment in Singapore due to leave restrictions. Another voter, Azizul Rahman, 30, said he plans to apply for annual leave much earlier for future elections.

items.title: AirBorneo to open ticket sales for Kuching-Singapore flights ahead of July 22 launch
items.summary: KUCHING, July 11 — AirBorneo Airways will begin selling tickets for its inaugural international service between Kuching and Singapore on July 13, ahead of the launch of daily flights on July 22, which coincides with the Hari Sarawak celebration. “The new daily service between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 will strengthen Sarawak’s connectivity with one of the region’s key aviation and business hubs, while opening new opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and Singapore,” the statement said. He said the Kuching-Singapore service marked a significant step in AirBorneo’s growth, extending Sarawak’s air connectivity beyond Malaysia’s borders for the first time.

items.title: Sarawak Immigration crackdown around Kuching, Kota Samarahan nets 24 foreign nationals
items.summary: KUCHING, July 11 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 24 foreign nationals during Op Minyak, Op Selera, and Op Mahir, which were carried out at seven locations around Kuching and Kota Samarahan yesterday. Inspections focused on heavy machinery workshops in Kampung Semeba and Kampung Sudat in Kuching, as well as eateries and premises suspected of conducting online gambling activities in Muara Tuang, Kota Samarahan. Several scrap metal and second-hand goods premises around Kota Samarahan were also inspected to ensure compliance with the country’s immigration laws.

items.title: Iran hits back at Trump after insists truce over
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran insisted on Saturday it had "kept its word" on a ceasefire with the United States, after President Donald Trump insisted the truce was over but that he had agreed to further negotiations with the Islamic republic. While there have been no direct talks between Iran and the United States since last month, Iranian media reported that a delegation from mediator Qatar was in Teheran after the sides exchanged strikes. "The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue 'talks.'

items.title: Johor election turnout reaches 1.13mil by 1pm
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout in the Johor election reached 1,130,980 as of 1pm today. The Election Commission (EC), in its hourly update, said 42.16 per cent of the more than 2.6 million eligible voters had cast their ballots. Early voting on Tuesday recorded a 95 per cent turnout, involving 19,471 voters.

items.title: LPT crash: More than 30 motorcycles believed involved in illegal racing, says police
items.summary: District police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said 18 victims were treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), while two others received treatment at Sultan Ahmad Shah Medical Centre here. Earlier, Ashari said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. He said another victim, Muhammad Safawi Md Noh, 24, died while receiving treatment at HTAA.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 42pc by 1pm, ahead of previous state election pace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.13 million voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). In a Facebook post, the EC said the Semarang and Sedili state constituencies recorded the highest voter turnout at 50.5 per cent each, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent. Penggaram registered the lowest turnout at 32.2 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 32.6 per cent and Simpang Jeram at 35.2 per cent.

items.title: Fahmi, Ramakrishnan, Soo among Penang state honours recipients
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil was today conferred the Darjah Panglima Pangkuan Negeri (DPPN), which carries the title 'Datuk Seri', in conjunction with the 2026 Penang state awards, medals, and honours investiture ceremony, held to mark the 85th birthday celebration of Penang Yang di-Pertua Negeri, Tun Ramli Ngah Talib. Among other notable recipients are Techninier Sdn Bhd chief executive officer, Datuk Peh Lai Yian—a corporate figure and entrepreneur with over 20 years of experience in the telecommunications, digital infrastructure, and utility digitalisation sectors; Datuk Soo Sze Ching, a corporate figure who has dedicated over 20 years of service; Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman; and Invest Penang CEO Datuk Loo Lee Lian. ), while 72 received the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (D.S.P.N.

items.title: BN's Md Jafni casts ballot, urges Johoreans to vote responsibly
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN) Bukit Permai candidate Datuk Md Jafni Md Shukor cast his vote with his wife and two children at Sekolah Kebangsaan Bandar Putra in Kulai today. Jafni urged Johoreans to fulfil their civic responsibility calmly and responsibly. Jafni also humbly sought the prayers, blessings and continued support of voters in Bukit Permai and Kulai as polling continued across the state.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: DAP determined to defend all 11 state seats
items.summary: SEREMBAN: DAP is determined to defend all 11 state seats it holds in the 16th Negri Sembilan election on Aug 1. Negri Sembilan DAP secretary Cha Kee Chin said the party would leverage its track record and public service performance to retain the seats. "We need to wrest the Paroi and Labu state seats.

items.title: PLKN 3.0: 250 female trainees report for national service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 250 female trainees from across the country reported for the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 3.0 (Series 3/2026) at the 515 Territorial Army (AW) Camp here today. National Service Training Department director (Planning and Policy Division) Colonel Abd Rani Abdul Majid said 12 other trainees were granted special permission to report tomorrow to allow them to vote in the Johor election today. PLKN 3.0 adopts a comprehensive training approach comprising 70 per cent military training and 30 per cent nation-building modules aimed at developing character, discipline, and identity in the younger generation.

items.title: 2 Indonesians held over alleged illegal fishing in Selangor waters
items.summary: PORT KLANG: A fibre boat with two Indonesians suspected of carrying out illegal fishing activities was detained by the Selangor Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) in Kapar waters during an enforcement operation last night. Selangor MMEA director Maritime Captain Abdul Muhaimin Muhammad Salleh said the boat was detained at about 10.30pm by the agency's patrol vessel 0.8 nautical miles southwest of Sungai Sementa Besar, Kapar, during routine patrols under an enforcement operation in Selangor waters. The boat skipper also failed to produce any licence or permit authorising fishing activities.

items.title: Johor polls: IGP says no candidates implicated in 90 election-related police reports
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Police received a total of 90 reports across Johor during the 14-day campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, which ended at midnight yesterday. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said none of the reports involved election candidates or senior leaders of the contesting political parties. He also urged voters with complaints or grievances related to the election process to lodge reports with the police to enable the Election Commission and other relevant agencies to take prompt action.

items.title: Heavy rain and thunderstorms lash Johor, but voters brave the weather to keep polling on track
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Heavy rain and thunderstorms across Johor since midday today have not dampened the spirit of voters turning out for the 16th Johor state election. A Bernama check at several polling centres in Muar found continuous rain since morning affecting Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (SJK) Cina Limbong and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sri Muar. In Johor Bahru, similar weather conditions at SMK Bandar Uda Utama also failed to deter voters from playing their part, with polling centres remaining calm and orderly.

items.title: Johor election: Journalists return home to cast ballots
items.summary: KLUANG: For many journalists covering the Johor election, today was not only about reporting on democracy but also participating in it. Despite being assigned to cover polling activities across the state, several reporters made time to return to their respective constituencies to fulfil their responsibility as voters. Utusan Malaysia reporter Hanafi Zulkefli, 25, travelled from Simpang Renggam to Tangkak after completing his reporting assignment in the morning, before resuming election coverage until the announcement of results tonight.

items.title: Smooth traffic at Johor Causeway, Second Link and main JB roads on polling day
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 – Traffic conditions on several major routes in Johor were reported to be free-flowing to moderate on polling day despite an increase in the number of vehicles following the return of voters from Singapore. Checks at the main south-bound routes at the Skudai toll plaza and Kulai toll plaza showed a smooth traffic flow towards the Johor Bahru city centre. Despite the increase in vehicles entering the Johor Bahru city centre, traffic was reported to be at a moderate flow towards the afternoon.

items.title: Smooth traffic at Johor checkpoints as voters head to polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic flow at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) and Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB) remains smooth as 2.6 million voters head to the polls for the 16th Johor state election today. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said maximum operations had been activated at both land entry points between Malaysia and Singapore to facilitate the return of voters. Based on updates from the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA), Saifuddin said special lanes, hybrid counters, and contra-flow lanes were deployed to manage the anticipated congestion.

items.title: Police receive 90 reports, open 25 investigation papers during Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police received 90 police reports and opened 25 investigation papers throughout the campaigning period for the 16th Johor election. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the reports and investigation papers involved minor offences that did not affect public order and were still under investigation. "We have opened 25 investigation papers, most of which involve minor offences that I believe will not cause concern or tension among voters or election personnel during this election.

items.title: Johor election: Age and disability fail to stop Johor voters casting ballots
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Losing his left leg to diabetes complications seven years ago did not stop Abd Karim Taha from making his way to the polling centre today to exercise his right as a voter in the Johor election. Accompanied by his three children, Nor Alia Maisarah, Nor Kamalia Amira and Abdul Luqman Hakim, aged 23, 25 and 28 respectively, Abd Karim arrived at SK LKTP Sungai Sayong despite his mobility challenges. However, Abd Karim became emotional when recalling former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak.

items.title: Johor polls: Heavy rain fails to deter voters
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Heavy rain and thunderstorms across Johor since noon today failed to dampen the spirit of voters turning out for the 16th Johor state election. Continuous morning rain affected several polling centres in Muar, including Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina Limbong and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Sri Muar. In Johor Baru, similar weather conditions at SMK Bandar Uda Utama also failed to deter voters, with polling remaining calm and orderly.

items.title: Johor polls: More than 709,000 voters cast ballots by 11am as turnout hits 26.43pc
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 709,000 voters, or 26.43 per cent of registered electors, had cast their ballots as of 11am today in the 16th Johor state election, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said. Ramlan said the turnout figure was recorded after polling began at 8am across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. The Johor state election sees more than 2.6 million registered voters casting their ballots to elect representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.

items.title: Man killed, three injured after car rams parked motorcycle, tree stump
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A 39-year-old driver was killed when his car lost control, rammed a parked motorcycle, and crashed into a tree stump before overturning at Jalan Mengabang Lekar in Kuala Nerus early today. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said police received information at 3.55am regarding the crash involving a Proton Saga and a Honda EX5 motorcycle. He said the Proton Saga was travelling from Merang, Setiu towards the driver's home in Kampung Maras, while the motorcycle was parked on the left shoulder of the road facing towards Mengabang Lekar from Kuala Terengganu town.

items.title: Bersatu still strong despite Perlis MB's resignation, says Muhyiddin
items.summary: MUAR: Bersatu remains strong despite the resignation of Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah and several of state leaders leaving the party, said its president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin. "He (Abu Bakar Hamzah) resigned as a member of the Supreme Council, but he is still a Bersatu member. Meanwhile, Muhyiddin said Bersatu remained intact despite several leaders resigning and leaving the party.

items.title: Bride hurries from wedding to cast vote in Johor polls [WATCH]
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Less than an hour after becoming a wife, Fifi Nur Khairin Tamrin, 24, hurried from her wedding reception to fulfil her civic duty by voting in the Johor election today. Fifi drew attention when she arrived at the polling centre at Sekolah Kebangsaan LKTP Sungai Sayong wearing a white wedding gown, accompanied by her husband, Muhammad Arif Fahmi Nor Azli, 28. Fifi said they had fixed their wedding date in August last year, never expecting it to coincide with polling day.

items.title: Store fire near city centre sends thick smoke billowing over Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A fire at a construction materials store in Stulang Laut sent thick, dark smoke billowing near the city centre today, though it did not disrupt the ongoing 16th Johor election. The Johor Fire and Rescue Department operations centre said it received an emergency call at 9.39am and dispatched teams from the Larkin and Tebrau fire stations to the scene near R&F Mall. Firefighting operations are still ongoing," the department said in a statement.

items.title: AirBorneo to open Kuching-Singapore ticket sales, fares from RM399 one-way
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirBorneo Airways Sdn Bhd (AirBorneo) will open ticket sales for its first international route between Kuching and Singapore on July 13 with all-in one-way economy class fares from RM399. The Sarawak-owned airline said the daily service would begin on July 22 and operate between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4. Business Times first reported on July 6 that AirBorneo had filed planned B737-800 flights from Kuching to Kuala Lumpur and Singapore in the OAG airline schedules database.

items.title: Johor voter turnout reaches 34.78pct by noon
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout in the Johor election reached 933,115 as of noon today. The Election Commission, in its hourly update, said 34.78 per cent of the more than 2.6 million eligible voters had voted. Early voting on Tuesday recorded a 95 per cent turnout, involving 19,471 voters.

items.title: Johor election: 103-year-old Kastawi proves age is no barrier to voting
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Being more than a century old did not stop Kastawi Esa from fulfilling his civic duty when he turned up at SK LKTP Sungai Sayong here to cast his ballot in the Johor election today. I am grateful that I am still healthy and able to fulfil my responsibility as a voter," he said. Meanwhile, Felda Sungai Sayong village head Shaiful Bahri Sidek, 60, said the village administration had arranged transportation to ensure Kastawi was able to vote.

items.title: First-time Johor voters want leaders who deliver, not just make promises
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: First-time voters in the 16th Johor state election today said they were looking beyond party affiliations, placing greater emphasis on candidates' integrity, commitment and ability to deliver on their promises. Many young voters who cast their ballots said they wanted elected representatives who could improve the lives of residents, remain accountable and produce tangible results, rather than make commitments during campaigning alone. Agriculture diploma student Ahmad Irfan Harith Ahmad Izwan, 19, said a strong sense of civic responsibility prompted him to arrive early at the polling station to avoid traffic congestion.

items.title: No ban on shorts at polling stations, says EC after voter allegedly stopped in Skudai
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — The Election Commission (EC) has clarified that there is no official dress code preventing voters from wearing shorts to polling stations, after an alleged incident involving a voter at a Skudai polling centre. “No such dress code. The images also showed the man speaking to a school security guard and a sign at the school gate listing a visitor dress code that prohibits male visitors from wearing shorts, ripped jeans, sleeveless tops and slippers.

items.title: Two teen boys held over vandalism spree that damaged 8 vehicles in Gerik
items.summary: GERIK: Police have arrested two teenage boys, aged 13 and 14, over a string of vandalism cases that left eight vehicles damaged with estimated losses of RM10,000. District police chief Superintendent Abdul Samad Othman said the boys were arrested early today, less than 24 hours after police received eight reports of smashed vehicle windows in Taman Sri Temenggor, Taman Meha and Taman Gerik. He said the pair were suspected of damaging three vehicles in Taman Sri Temenggor yesterday.

items.title: Johor election: 26.43 per cent voter turnout as of 11am
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout in the Johor state election reached 709,092 as of 11am today. Early voting on Tuesday recorded a turnout of 95 per cent, involving 19,471 voters. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Teo says MCMC received 29 complaints over online content during Johor campaign
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) received 29 complaints related to online content involving fake news, hate speech and scams during the campaign period for the 16th Johor state election. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said 17 of the complaints were related to the dissemination of fake news, while 11 involved hate speech and one concerned a fake account and impersonation. The 16th Johor state election sees 172 candidates contesting 56 State Legislative Assembly seats, with more than 2.6 million registered voters deciding the outcome today.

items.title: Penang TYT calls for expedited construction of new ceremonial hall at Seri Mutiara
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib hopes that efforts to construct a ceremonial hall (Dewan Istiadat) at the Seri Mutiara official residence will be expedited. He said a proper ceremonial hall was crucial to ensure that state investiture ceremonies are conducted with appropriate decorum, tradition, and grandeur. Nine people received the Darjah Mulia Pangkuan Negeri (DMPN), while 103 received the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (DSPN), both carrying the title Datuk.

items.title: Penang governor hopes federal govt backs plan for Ceremonial Hall at Seri Mutiara
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 11 — Penang Yang Dipertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib has expressed hope that the federal government will give due consideration to a proposal to build a Ceremonial Hall within the Seri Mutiara grounds to enhance the conduct of official state ceremonies and uphold the dignity of the institution of the Head of State. He said such a facility is essential to ensure that investiture ceremonies for Penang’s state orders, decorations and medals are conducted with the appropriate decorum, tradition and grandeur. The governor said the proposed Ceremonial Hall would enable the state government to better fulfil its responsibility to uphold the dignity, honour and sovereignty of Penang.

items.title: Over 30 motorcycles involved in LPT crash that killed 4, injured 20
items.summary: KUANTAN: At least 20 people were injured, and more than 30 motorcycles were involved in the fatal highway crash that killed four motorcyclists at Km 246.4 of the East Coast Highway (LPT) today. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah confirmed the updated figures following the incident, which occurred near the Jabor toll plaza exit. "A total of 20 victims have been confirmed injured and are currently receiving treatment at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) and the Sultan Ahmad Shah Hospital, International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM).

items.title: Bangsar loses a beloved gem as Restoran Berjaya to close after 45 years [WATCH]
items.summary: In a statement, the family-run restaurant announced its closure on Facebook and said the decision was not an easy one, but rising rental costs and the realities of old age had led them to bring the restaurant's long journey to a close. One patron, Liza Salim, said she often craved the restaurant's mihun curry after spending 2.5 years studying overseas. she said.

items.title: #NSTviral: World Cup banter: MAS joins Norway-England airline rivalry with cheeky social media post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines has joined the FIFA World Cup fever with a cheeky social media response to an airline rivalry between Norwegian Air and British Airways. The exchange began when Norwegian Air challenged British Airways to switch their Instagram logos if Norway beats England in the quarter-finals scheduled for 5am on Sunday (Malaysian time). Tagging British Airways, Norwegian Air posted: "Hey, @british_airways, do you want to make a bet?

items.title: Father, son seriously injured in 10-vehicle pileup on AMJ Highway
items.summary: State Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Superintendent Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the 9pm accident involved seven cars, a lorry, a four-wheel-drive vehicle, and the Honda Dash motorcycle carrying the father and son. He said the 39-year-old motorcyclist suffered a broken leg, while his son sustained serious head injuries and required intubation after their motorcycle was struck from behind. The 29-year-old Toyota Hilux driver suffered minor facial injuries," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Johor polls: PH's Nur Hafiz Roslan casts vote in former school, praises encouraging turnout
items.summary: KLUANG: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Machap, Nur Hafiz Roslan, cast his ballot at Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Melayu here at 9.15am today. Wearing a maroon shirt, Nur Hafiz arrived at the polling centre accompanied by his mother. Earlier, Nur Hafiz said he was encouraged by the long queues and voter enthusiasm since early morning.

items.title: Johor polls: MCMC receives 29 complaints over fake news, hate speech
items.summary: KULAI: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) received 29 complaints involving the spread of fake news, hate speech and fraud during the Johor election. She said that of the 29 complaints, 17 involved the spread of fake news, while 11 were related to hate speech and one involved an online fraud case. "Throughout the campaign period from June 1 to July 9, 2026, MCMC received 29 complaints related to fake news, hate speech and fraud that occurred during the campaign.

items.title: Johor polls see 7.97pc turnout by 9am, surpassing previous election pace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11— A total of 213,878 voters, or 7.97 per cent of the more than 2.6 million voters, had cast their ballots as of 9 am in the 16th Johor state election today. The Election Commission (EC), in a Facebook post, said the Mahkota state constituency recorded the highest voter turnout at 15.8 per cent, followed by Tanjung Surat (14.7 per cent) and Pasir Raja (13.6 per cent). Meanwhile, Bentayan and Simpang Jeram recorded the lowest voter turnout, with both registering 7.7 per cent, while Kukup recorded 7.6 per cent.

items.title: EC chairman makes rounds of Johor polling centres as voting continues smoothly
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun today inspected the voting process at four polling centres across the city to ensure the Johor state election was proceeding smoothly and in an orderly manner. He began his inspection at the Dewan Raya Taman Ungku Tun Aminah polling centre for the Skudai constituency, which has seven polling streams, at about 8.50 am. Ramlan then proceeded to the Raja Zarith Sofiah Library at Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) for the Senai constituency, which has two polling streams, followed by Sekolah Agama Taman Bukit Mewah in the Kempas constituency (six polling streams), before concluding his visit at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Bandar Uda Utama in the Perling constituency, which has nine polling streams.

items.title: Johor polls: Ministers, deputy ministers among early voters as state chooses new govt
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Several ministers and deputy ministers cast their ballots in the 16th Johor State Election this morning, exercising their rights as registered voters. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin exercised his voting rights at 8.30 am at Sekolah Kebangsaan Rinting 1, Masai, here. Deputy Works Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan cast his ballot at 8.40 am at Sekolah Kebangsaan Seri Bahagia, Benut.

items.title: Four Yamaha RXZ riders killed in 13-vehicle crash on East Coast Expressway
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 11 — Four motorcyclists were killed and several others injured in a 13-vehicle crash at KM246.4 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) near here early today. Kuantan police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. Preliminary investigations found that the car, driven by a 25-year-old man from Kuantan, was travelling towards Kemaman when the crash occurred.

items.title: Onn Hafiz votes early in Simpang Renggam, urges Johoreans to turn out
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi cast his vote in the 16th Johor state election at Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Simpang Renggam at 8.33 am today. Before entering Stream 4 to cast his ballot, Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, took time to mingle with and greet other voters waiting for their turn. Seeking a second term, Onn Hafiz faces a straight fight against Nur Hafiz Roslan of Pakatan Harapan (PH).

items.title: Johor polls open with clear skies across most areas, but heavy rain dampens Muar
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Polling for the 16th Johor State Election began this morning under generally favourable weather conditions, with clear skies reported in most areas except for Muar, which experienced heavy rain from the early hours. A Bernama check at several polling centres found Election Commission (EC) officials, security personnel and media practitioners on site as early as 7am. Similar weather conditions were reported at Dewan Raya Taman Ungku Tun Aminah, Johor Bahru; Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Senggarang, Batu Pahat; and Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina (SJKC) Kulai.

items.title: MACC freezes 14 bank accounts, seizes RM1.4m assets in foreign affairs syndicate probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has frozen 14 bank accounts, comprising five company accounts and nine personal accounts belonging to several individuals, involving an estimated RM1.1 million, following investigations into a syndicate suspected of committing various offences related to foreign nationals affairs. “Yesterday, MACC also detained another enforcement officer at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya to assist in investigations. On Wednesday, through Op Layer (MACC Putrajaya), Op Dalang (MACC Selangor), Op Raider (MACC Negeri Sembilan) and Op Genuine (MACC Melaka), 38 individuals were arrested, with 33 remanded to assist in investigations.

items.title: Abang Johari says Sibu must move beyond timber industry with new economy
items.summary: SIBU, July 11 — Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg wants Sibu to be transformed into a low-carbon smart city driven by new technology, a green economy, and development based on a master plan to ensure the city remains competitive in the future. “Sibu must be developed according to its master plan. Abang Johari said that the development of Sibu will be driven through the Mid-Rajang Central Region Development Agency (MiRRDA) with the support of the Rajang Delta Development Agency (RADDA), which each received an allocation of RM1.5 billion to implement projects according to local needs through a bottom-up development approach.

items.title: Rafizi says Bersama aims to break Malaysia’s race-based politics with multiracial platform
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) sees the Johor state election as an opportunity for voters to move away from divisive race-based politics towards a more united platform for Malaysia’s future, said Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. The Bersama co-leader said he hoped the party and its candidates could win support from Malaysians of all races, reflecting the country’s diversity, in today’s polls. Also present was Bersama co-leader Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and the party’s 15 candidates contesting various seats across Johor.

items.title: Vigneswaran confident MIC can retain Indian support in Johor election
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran is confident that the Indian community will continue to support the Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in the Johor state election tomorrow, especially in the seats contested by MIC. He said that the confidence was based on the good relationship between the MIC and both the federal and state governments in addressing and resolving various issues involving the Indian community. The 16th Johor state election will see four candidates representing the MIC, namely K. Raven Kumar in the Kemelah state assembly seat; V. Rugendran (Kahang); P. Pannir Selvam (Perling); and R. Kumaran (Bukit Batu).

items.title: Anwar hits back at Sanusi over continued attacks despite Kedah development efforts
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, July 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed disappointment over Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor’s continued political attacks against him despite the Federal Government’s ongoing efforts to drive development in the state. When I went to Kedah, he did not even turn up, yet we brought the Thai Prime Minister there (to Bukit Kayu Hitam), not to Kuala Lumpur but to that remote area,” he said when speaking at the PH Grand Finale programme for the Layang-Layang state constituency here last night. He said bringing Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul to Bukit Kayu Hitam yesterday was aimed at officiating the opening of a new road alignment as part of efforts to boost the border economy, thereby benefiting the people of Perlis and Kedah.

items.title: Zahid assures Johor polls outcome will not affect federal govt stability
items.summary: KULAI, July 11 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has given an assurance that whatever the outcome of the 16th Johor State Election today, it will not affect the stability and relationship among political parties at the federal government level. BN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi stressed that the federal administration has so far been functioning as usual and expressed hope that the momentum of harmony would continue without any disruption. The Deputy Prime Minister said ministers and deputy ministers in the federal government have consistently demonstrated professionalism in carrying out their responsibilities without being influenced by political competition in Johor.

items.title: Anwar’s last push urges Johor voters to back govt that protects people’s interests
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim tonight spearheaded the final push of Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) campaign for the 16th Johor State Election (PRN) through the ‘Grand Finale Johor Ke Depan: Undi Harapan’ programme in Pasir Gudang, just hours before the campaigning period concludes at 11:59 pm last night. Among those in attendance were Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) President Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, who is also the Minister of Agriculture and Food Security; PKR Vice President and Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari; and DAP Secretary-General and Transport Minister Anthony Loke. Merahkan Johor!

items.title: All Johor polling stations open at 8am as state election gets under way
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — A total of 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams for the Johor state election opened simultaneously at 8 am today, allowing voters across the state to elect a new government for the next term. The election sees 172 candidates vying for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats, with 12 four-cornered contests, 27 three-cornered contests and 14 straight fights. For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor.

items.title: All eyes on turnout as Johor heads to polls today
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Johor voters head to the polls today in what analysts say will be a closely watched test not only of Barisan Nasional’s (BN) grip on the state, but also of voter enthusiasm ahead of a series of elections expected over the coming years. While BN is widely expected to retain the state government, analysts say the coalition’s margin of victory, voter turnout and the performances of smaller parties could shape the political narrative long after the ballots are counted. Turnout remains the biggest question Political analysts agree that voter turnout will be the single most important factor to watch as polling unfolds.

items.title: Anwar tells Johor election rivals to stop using Najib’s imprisonment as campaign issue
items.summary: KULAI, July 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded parties contesting in the Johor state election to stop using the issue of Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s release as political campaign fodder. “Datuk (Seri) Najib is now in prison, so leave him alone. Merahkan Johor!

items.title: Immigration Dept: 81 officers disciplined over abuse of power, corruption since January
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 10 — A total of 81 Immigration Department officers and rank-and-file personnel faced disciplinary action for abuse of power and corruption between Jan 1 and June 30 this year. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said out of that number, 14 individuals were dismissed from service. “This shows that we are indeed serious about addressing integrity issues among immigration officers.

items.title: Two men with criminal records detained over Petaling Jaya hotel murder probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — Police have arrested two local men to assist in the investigation of the murder of a man who was found dead at a hotel in Sunway Mentari, Petaling Jaya, last Wednesday. Petaling Jaya District Police Chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said that both suspects, in their 20s and 30s, were detained following initial information received by the police at 11:23 pm regarding an unconscious local man at the hotel. “Upon arrival at the scene, police found the victim, in his 30s, lifeless.

items.title: Blogger fined RM11,000 over offensive TikTok post targeting Ismail Sabri, Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 10 — The Sessions Court here fined blogger Jufazli Shi Ahmad RM11,000 today after finding him guilty of transmitting offensive communications against former Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob and UMNO four years ago. “After evaluating the entire defence and the witnesses’ testimonies, the court finds Jufazli guilty and convicts him as charged. Jufazli paid the fine today.
